tests.label: CFT-001-ping
tests.description: Calls ping() five times in sequence and asserts every call returns true. Verifies idempotency and the absence of resource leaks across repeated calls.
tests.notes: Execute calls in a simple for-loop. Lightweight smoke-test for connection-pool or socket reuse issues.

tests.label: CFT-002-ping
tests.description: Performs a createFile operation then immediately calls ping(). Verifies that ping remains available and correct after the service has processed real work.
tests.notes: Skipped via Assume.assumeNotNull if -Dlzname and -Dlztestfile system properties are not provided. createFile failure is acceptable; only ping result is asserted.

tests.label: ECT-001-ping
tests.description: Submits 10 concurrent ping() calls using an ExecutorService and asserts all return true. Verifies thread-safety and correct concurrent handling by the Axis2 stub.
tests.notes: Uses Executors.newFixedThreadPool(10) and invokeAll with a 30-second timeout. Fails if any thread receives false or throws.

tests.label: EHT-001-ping
tests.description: Creates an HPCCWsFileIOClient pointing at RFC 5737 TEST-NET host 192.0.2.1:8010 and asserts ping() returns false. Verifies ConnectException is swallowed and not propagated.
tests.notes: Uses a 2-second connection timeout to avoid long TCP timeout waits. 192.0.2.1 is RFC 5737 TEST-NET guaranteed unreachable.

tests.label: EHT-002-ping
tests.description: Creates an HPCCWsFileIOClient pointing at the correct HPCC host but port 9999 and asserts ping() returns false. Verifies connection-refused error is handled gracefully.
tests.notes: Extracts host from hpccconn system property and substitutes port 9999. Uses 2-second connect timeout.

tests.label: EHT-003-ping
tests.description: Documents the design constraint that HPCCWsFileIOClient cannot be constructed without a valid Connection; verifyStub() guard is unreachable via the public API.
tests.notes: Annotated @Ignore. HPCCWsFileIOClient has no no-arg constructor; passing null Connection throws NullPointerException at construction time before ping() is called.

tests.label: CNT-001-ping
tests.description: Creates an HPCCWsFileIOClient with correct endpoint but wrong credentials and asserts ping() returns false. Verifies auth failure is handled gracefully on a secured cluster.
tests.notes: Skipped on unsecured clusters via Assume.assumeTrue(client.doesTargetHPCCAuthenticate()). Uses username 'invalid_user' and password 'wrong_password'.

tests.label: CNT-002-ping
tests.description: Creates an HPCCWsFileIOClient with empty username and password against a secured cluster and asserts ping() returns false. Validates absent credentials are treated as auth failure.
tests.notes: Skipped on unsecured clusters via Assume.assumeTrue(client.doesTargetHPCCAuthenticate()). Complements CNT-001 by covering the empty-credential edge case.

tests.label: CFT-001-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Creates a new file with overwrite=false when the file does not pre-exist. Verifies the operation succeeds and returns true.
tests.notes: Unique timestamped file name guarantees the file does not pre-exist. Uses overwrite=false on a new file which should succeed.

tests.label: CFT-002-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Creates a file and then creates it again with overwrite=true. Verifies both calls succeed and no exception is thrown.
tests.notes: Both calls should return true. The second call silently overwrites the file.

tests.label: CFT-003-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Invokes the deprecated 3-parameter createHPCCFile overload (no lzAddress). Verifies it delegates to the 4-parameter version with lzAddress=null and returns true in a containerized environment.
tests.notes: Skipped on non-containerized clusters. Validates backward compatibility of the deprecated API.

tests.label: CFT-004-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Passes lzAddress=null explicitly with the 4-parameter signature in a containerized environment. Verifies DestNetAddress is omitted from the request and the server accepts the call.
tests.notes: Skipped on non-containerized clusters. Distinct from AcreateHPCCFile because it is an explicit null-address test without environment branching.

tests.label: ECT-001-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Uses a fileName containing a subdirectory path component. Verifies the method handles path separators without throwing an unexpected exception.
tests.notes: Outcome (true/false/exception) depends on whether the subdirectory pre-exists on the LZ. Test passes as long as no unchecked exception escapes.

tests.label: ECT-002-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Uses a fileName with no file extension. Verifies the server does not enforce an extension requirement and the file is created successfully.
tests.notes: Tests that the server accepts plain file names without an extension.

tests.label: ECT-003-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Uses a fileName containing hyphens and underscores. Verifies common separator characters are accepted by the server.
tests.notes: Hyphens and underscores are valid POSIX filename characters and should be accepted.

tests.label: ECT-004-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Passes an empty string as lzAddress. Verifies the client treats it identically to null and does not include DestNetAddress in the request.
tests.notes: Client code: if (lzAddress != null && !lzAddress.isEmpty()). Empty string is explicitly excluded, same as null. Only valid on containerized.

tests.label: ECT-005-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Uses a fileName of 255 characters to test the upper boundary of file system and server name length limits. Verifies no unchecked exception escapes.
tests.notes: Both true/false and expected exception types are acceptable outcomes. Test passes if no unchecked exception escapes.

tests.label: ECT-006-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Uses a single-character fileName ("x") to test the minimal lower boundary of the DestRelativePath field. Verifies the server accepts it.
tests.notes: A single ASCII character is a valid filename on all supported platforms.

tests.label: EHT-001-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Passes null as fileName. Verifies the client throws an Exception with a message containing 'fileName required' before making any network call.
tests.notes: Client-side validation fires before any network call. No server contact should occur.

tests.label: EHT-002-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Passes empty string as fileName. Verifies the client throws an Exception with a message containing 'fileName required' before any network call.
tests.notes: Empty string triggers the same client-side guard as null.

tests.label: EHT-003-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Passes null as targetLandingZone. Verifies the client throws an Exception with a message containing 'targetLandingZone required' before any network call.
tests.notes: targetLandingZone is validated before fileName in the current implementation.

tests.label: EHT-004-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Passes empty string as targetLandingZone. Verifies the client throws an Exception with a message containing 'targetLandingZone required'.
tests.notes: Empty string triggers the same client-side guard as null for targetLandingZone.

tests.label: EHT-005-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Creates a file then attempts to create the same file again with overwrite=false. Verifies the second call returns false, reflecting the server response 'Failure: <path> exists.'
tests.notes: First call uses overwrite=true to ensure the file exists. Represents the most common user error. Pre-condition failure skips via Assume.

tests.label: EHT-006-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Passes a fileName of '.' which resolves to the landing zone base directory. Verifies the server returns a failure result and the method returns false or throws an ESP exception.
tests.notes: Behaviour depends on how the HPCC server resolves '.' relative to the drop zone base path. Both false return and ESP exception are valid outcomes.

tests.label: EHT-007-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Passes a targetLandingZone that does not correspond to any configured drop zone. Verifies either an ArrayOfEspExceptionWrapper is thrown or the method returns false.
tests.notes: No silent success allowed. Documents whether the server returns a 'Fail...' result string or raises an ESP exception via validateDropZoneAccess.

tests.label: EHT-008-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Passes an unreachable lzAddress (RFC 5737 TEST-NET 192.0.2.1). Verifies the server surfaces an error with no silent success. Only meaningful for bare-metal deployments.
tests.notes: Skipped on containerized clusters. DestNetAddress is only used in baremetal mode for routing to the drop zone node.

tests.label: CNT-001-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Confirms the createHPCCFile endpoint is reachable and the method returns true for a minimal valid request against a live cluster.
tests.notes: Complements the generic ping() connectivity test with a method-specific connectivity check.

tests.label: CNT-002-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Calls createHPCCFile using a client configured with invalid credentials. Verifies an authentication-related exception is thrown or the method does not return true.
tests.notes: Skipped when doesTargetHPCCAuthenticate() returns false. Test passes if exception is thrown OR method returns false — must not return true.

tests.label: CNT-003-createHPCCFile
tests.description: Calls createHPCCFile on a secured HPCC cluster using valid credentials. Verifies the permission check passes and the method returns true.
tests.notes: Skipped when doesTargetHPCCAuthenticate() returns false. Validates the HPCC permission grant path for FILE_IO_URL.

tests.label: CFT-001-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Write a small ASCII text payload using the primary non-deprecated overload with an explicit dataTypeDescriptor of 'text/plain; charset=UTF-8'. Verifies the method returns true and the data can be read back.
tests.notes: Exercises the primary non-deprecated overload; verifies MIME type is accepted and round-trip read-back matches.

tests.label: CFT-002-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Call the primary overload with dataTypeDescriptor=null. Verifies that the client defaults to 'application/octet-stream' internally and the write succeeds without a NullPointerException.
tests.notes: Validates the null-guard on dataTypeDescriptor in HPCCWsFileIOClient.

tests.label: CFT-003-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Call the primary overload with dataTypeDescriptor='' (empty string). Verifies the internal isEmpty() guard defaults to 'application/octet-stream' and the write succeeds.
tests.notes: Exercises the isEmpty() branch of the MIME type null-guard.

tests.label: CFT-004-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Write a byte array containing non-printable binary bytes (simulated PNG header) and verify round-trip correctness via readFileData. Validates MTOM/MIME encoding preserves binary content.
tests.notes: Uses ISO-8859-1 for byte comparison since readFileData returns a Java String.

tests.label: CFT-005-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Write a JSON string with dataTypeDescriptor='application/json' and verify the server accepts it. Tests client-side DataSource construction with a non-standard MIME type.
tests.notes: Server-side storage is MIME-agnostic; primarily tests client DataSource construction.

tests.label: CFT-006-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Write a 100-byte payload with uploadchunksize=20, forcing 5 loop iterations. Verifies the full data is written correctly via readFileData length check.
tests.notes: Directly exercises the while(bytesleft > 0) loop for multiple iterations.

tests.label: CFT-007-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass uploadchunksize=0 and uploadchunksize=-1 to verify the client substitutes defaultUploadChunkSize (5,000,000) and writes succeed without arithmetic errors.
tests.notes: Two writes in one test method (cft007a and cft007b); both must return true.

tests.label: CFT-008-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Create a file, write known content with chunksize=5 (multiple chunks), read it back, and assert byte-level equality. Definitive correctness end-to-end test.
tests.notes: Small chunksize=5 exercises multi-chunk correctness and data ordering simultaneously.

tests.label: ECT-001-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Set uploadchunksize exactly equal to data.length so the loop executes exactly once. Verifies correct single-chunk boundary behaviour.
tests.notes: Boundary condition: chunksize == data.length == 10 bytes.

tests.label: ECT-002-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Set uploadchunksize larger than data.length (chunk exceeds payload). Loop runs exactly once; verifies no ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.
tests.notes: 9-byte data with chunksize=100.

tests.label: ECT-003-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Write a single-byte payload (0x42). Verifies the minimum valid non-empty write is handled correctly.
tests.notes: Minimum boundary for non-empty data; uploadchunksize=1.

tests.label: ECT-004-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: In a bare-metal environment, provide an explicit lzAddress from system property. Verify the client sets DestNetAddress in the request and the write succeeds.
tests.notes: Skipped in containerized environments via assumeFalse(isContainerized).

tests.label: ECT-005-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Write approximately 1 MB of data with a 100 KB chunk size (~10 iterations). Verify all bytes are written without timeout or exception.
tests.notes: Tests chunked loop performance. Optional readFileData confirmation omitted due to ESP response size limits.

tests.label: ECT-006-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass append=false and document that the client overrides this to append=true in the loop (Note A bug). Both writes produce concatenated content 'FIRSTSECOND' (11 bytes).
tests.notes: Intentional bug documentation test. When bug is fixed, expected read-back should be 'SECOND' (6 bytes) and test will need updating.

tests.label: ECT-007-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass offset=5 and document that the client loop overrides it to data.length (Note A bug). Write still succeeds since append=true causes the server to ignore Offset.
tests.notes: Intentional bug documentation test; exists as a regression marker for Note A offset override.

tests.label: EHT-001-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass data=null. Expect NullPointerException at data.length before any server call is made.
tests.notes: Client-side exception; no network call made.

tests.label: EHT-002-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass data=new byte[0]. bytesleft=0 so the loop never runs; method returns true without any server communication. Documents silent success edge case.
tests.notes: Loop body never entered when data is empty; no server call made.

tests.label: EHT-003-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass fileName=null. Server returns 'Destination path not specified'; client does not detect this as an error and returns true. Documents client-side bug.
tests.notes: Intentional client bug documentation. If bug is fixed, expect false instead of true.

tests.label: EHT-004-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass fileName='' (empty string). Same server-side validation failure as EHT-003; client returns true due to same bug.
tests.notes: Parallel to EHT-003; exercises empty-string path.

tests.label: EHT-005-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass targetLandingZone=null. Server returns 'Destination not specified'; client returns true (bug).
tests.notes: Documents server-side error not surfaced as client-side failure.

tests.label: EHT-006-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass targetLandingZone='' (empty string). Server returns 'Destination not specified'; client returns true (bug).
tests.notes: Parallel to EHT-005.

tests.label: EHT-007-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Call writeHPCCFileData without first calling createHPCCFile. Server returns '<path> does not exist.'; client returns true (bug).
tests.notes: Documents that the client silently ignores 'file not found'. If bug is fixed, expect false.

tests.label: EHT-008-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Provide invalid lzAddress '999.999.999.999' in a bare-metal environment. Server's validateDropZoneAccess should reject the request.
tests.notes: Skipped in containerized environments via assumeFalse(isContainerized).

tests.label: EHT-009-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Construct an HPCCWsFileIOClient with an invalid host/port and call writeHPCCFileData. Expect an Exception from verifyStub() or the network layer.
tests.notes: Uses HPCCWsFileIOClient.get() factory with 'invalid.host.invalid:9999'.

tests.label: EHT-010-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Attempt to write using a client with deliberately wrong credentials on a security-enabled cluster. Server should reject with an authorization error.
tests.notes: Skipped via assumeTrue(client.doesTargetHPCCAuthenticate()) on unsecured clusters.

tests.label: CNT-001-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Confirm the WsFileIO service is reachable by executing a valid minimal writeHPCCFileData call. No AxisFault, ConnectException, or UnknownHostException should be thrown.
tests.notes: Distinct from existing ping test — exercises the full WriteFileData SOAP operation path.

tests.label: CNT-002-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Confirm that a write request using the configured valid credentials succeeds on a security-enabled cluster.
tests.notes: Skipped via assumeTrue(client.doesTargetHPCCAuthenticate()) on unsecured clusters.

tests.label: CNT-003-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Confirm that a write request with bad credentials is rejected at the authentication layer on a security-enabled cluster.
tests.notes: Equivalent to EHT-010 from auth perspective; included for connectivity matrix completeness.

tests.label: CFT-001-readFileData
tests.description: Reads 10 bytes starting at offset 5 from a known test file. Verifies partial reads with a non-zero offset return the correct byte range.
tests.notes: Uses landing zone file created in-test via createHPCCFile + writeHPCCFileData with READ_FILE_DATA_TEST_CONTENT. Expected response: bytes 5-14 of test string.

tests.label: CFT-002-readFileData
tests.description: Requests more bytes than remain from a given offset; verifies the server clamps the read to available data and returns without error.
tests.notes: Validates server-side clamping: dataToRead = min(dataSize, fileSize - offset). Offset=60, dataSize=1000.

tests.label: CFT-003-readFileData
tests.description: Requests exactly fileSize bytes from offset 0; verifies complete file content is returned when dataSize equals the file's total byte count.
tests.notes: Boundary condition: dataSize == fileSize. Full content should be returned and match the original data byte-for-byte.

tests.label: CFT-004-readFileData
tests.description: Reads a file in three sequential chunks and verifies that concatenating the chunks reconstructs the original file content exactly.
tests.notes: Simulates a paging pattern. Validates no data duplication or gaps across chunk boundaries.

tests.label: CFT-005-readFileData
tests.description: Reads a 1 MB file in a single call to verify no timeout or memory issue occurs with large payloads over MTOM/SOAP.
tests.notes: File is created in-test with 1 MB of repeating ASCII pattern. Tests client/server capacity for large binary payloads.

tests.label: ECT-001-readFileData
tests.description: Reads exactly 1 byte from offset 0 (minimum valid dataSize). Verifies the first byte of the test file is returned correctly.
tests.notes: Boundary condition: minimum valid dataSize. Expected response is 'H' (first character of test data).

tests.label: ECT-002-readFileData
tests.description: Reads from the last valid byte position (offset = fileSize - 1) with dataSize=1. Verifies the final byte is returned correctly.
tests.notes: Boundary condition: highest valid offset. Expected response is the last character of the test data string.

tests.label: ECT-003-readFileData
tests.description: Explicitly provides dropzoneAddress on a bare-metal cluster. Verifies setting DestNetAddress does not break the request.
tests.notes: Skipped on containerized clusters via assumeFalse. DestNetAddress was added in service version 1.01.

tests.label: ECT-004-readFileData
tests.description: Passes null for dropzoneAddress on a containerized cluster; verifies DestNetAddress is omitted from the request and the read succeeds.
tests.notes: Skipped on non-containerized clusters via assumeTrue. Validates the null-guard branch in the Java client.

tests.label: ECT-005-readFileData
tests.description: Passes an empty string for dropzoneAddress; the Java client treats it the same as null and the read succeeds on a containerized cluster.
tests.notes: Skipped on non-containerized clusters. Exercises the !dropzoneAddress.isEmpty() guard in the client.

tests.label: ECT-006-readFileData
tests.description: Writes a file containing bytes 0x00-0x7F and reads it back. Verifies binary data round-trips faithfully through the DataHandler/MTOM pipeline.
tests.notes: Uses bytes 0x00-0x7F (ASCII range) to avoid charset encoding issues. Comparison uses ISO_8859_1.

tests.label: EHT-001-readFileData
tests.description: Passes an empty string for dropzone; server returns 'Destination not specified' and the Java client throws an exception.
tests.notes: No file setup needed; server validates empty dropzone before file access. Expects exception containing 'Destination not specified'.

tests.label: EHT-002-readFileData
tests.description: Passes null for dropzone; verifies the call does not silently succeed and an exception is thrown.
tests.notes: Accepts any exception type. Method must not silently return without throwing.

tests.label: EHT-003-readFileData
tests.description: Passes an empty string for fileName; server returns 'Destination path not specified' and the Java client throws an exception.
tests.notes: No file setup needed. Expects exception containing 'Destination path not specified'.

tests.label: EHT-004-readFileData
tests.description: Passes dataSize=0; server validates dataSize less than 1 and returns 'Invalid data size.'
tests.notes: File created in-test to pass earlier dropzone/path validations. Expects exception containing 'Invalid data size.'.

tests.label: EHT-005-readFileData
tests.description: Passes dataSize=-1; server validates dataSize less than 1 and returns 'Invalid data size.'
tests.notes: File created in-test. Expects exception containing 'Invalid data size.'.

tests.label: EHT-006-readFileData
tests.description: Passes offset=-1; server returns 'Invalid offset.' and the Java client throws an exception.
tests.notes: File created in-test. Expects exception containing 'Invalid offset.'.

tests.label: EHT-007-readFileData
tests.description: Passes offset equal to the file size (first invalid position); server returns 'Invalid offset: file size = N.'
tests.notes: offset is set dynamically to data.length. Expects exception containing 'Invalid offset:'.

tests.label: EHT-008-readFileData
tests.description: Passes offset far beyond the file size; server returns 'Invalid offset: file size = N.'
tests.notes: offset=999999. Expects exception containing 'Invalid offset:'.

tests.label: EHT-009-readFileData
tests.description: Attempts to read a file that does not exist on the dropzone; server returns '<path> does not exist.'
tests.notes: Uses a timestamp-based unique filename guaranteed not to exist. Expects exception containing 'does not exist.'.

tests.label: EHT-010-readFileData
tests.description: Passes a dropzone name that does not correspond to any registered landing zone; server rejects during validateDropZoneAccess.
tests.notes: Accepts any exception type. Exact error message may vary by HPCC version.

tests.label: CNT-001-readFileData
tests.description: Confirms the readFileData endpoint is reachable by making a minimal valid request; verifies no transport-layer exception is thrown.
tests.notes: Distinct from ping() — confirms the specific readFileData SOAP operation is routed and handled. Fails only on transport-level connectivity errors.

tests.label: CNT-002-readFileData
tests.description: Attempts readFileData with invalid credentials on a secured cluster; verifies an authentication-related exception is thrown.
tests.notes: Skipped if cluster does not enforce authentication via doesTargetHPCCAuthenticate().

tests.label: CNT-003-readFileData
tests.description: Attempts readFileData with empty credentials on a secured cluster; verifies an authentication error is returned.
tests.notes: Skipped if cluster does not enforce authentication. Complements CNT-002 with empty credentials case.
